blocking_issues.title: loadSnapshot race condition: stale snapshot overwrites current version with no indicator
blocking_issues.description: The plans claim the store is monotonic and that mid-mutation renders reuse cached values — but that mitigation does not match the code. The early-return guard (14b auditReadinessStore.ts lines 678-700) fires only after a fetch resolves and writes to the cache. Two concurrent calls — version=1 and version=2 — both see an empty cache for their session, both fire fetchAuditReadiness(), and the late-resolver wins. A slow v=1 fetch resolving after a fast v=2 fetch writes {composition_version:1} over the v=2 snapshot. The response carries the composition_version the backend computed at request time; when v=1 overwrites v=2, the panel asserts 'Audit ready ✓' against a stale composition with no visible indicator.
blocking_issues.system_impact: For an audit-readiness surface, 'stale-but-no-indicator' is a correctness failure: the panel makes a definitive claim ('Audit ready') that is false relative to the current composition. The ELSPETH audit primacy requirement demands that what the panel asserts reflects the actual pipeline under evaluation.
blocking_issues.failure_mode: Silent wrong data. No error is surfaced. The user proceeds under a false assurance.
blocking_issues.feedback_loop_risk: Reinforcing: rapid edits advance the version quickly; each edit fires a new fetch; the probability of a stale resolver winning increases with validation latency. If validation is slow (LLM solver involved in the backend path), this race is nearly guaranteed to fire during active composition.
recommended_fixes: Track in-flight requests by sessionId; if a newer version's fetch completes first, suppress any earlier-version resolution from writing the cache. Key: record the 'max version requested so far' and skip writes from lower-version responses.

In the resolution arm, before writing the cache, re-read the store's compositionVersion for the session and skip the write if the fetched snapshot's composition_version is less than the current version.

Pass compositionVersion as a query parameter to the GET route; have the backend respond 409 Conflict if the session's DB version has advanced; the client treats 409 as a no-op and retries when the version settles.

blocking_issues.title: Phase 2C deletes handleValidate with no mechanical gate on Phase 3A Task 8 landing first
blocking_issues.description: Phase 2C Task 8 deletes handleValidate, injectSystemMessage, and sendValidationFeedback from InspectorPanel.tsx, stating 'Phase 3A Task 8 already does this' (14c-phase-2c-frontend-integration.md, Task 8 description and Risks table). Phase 3A Task 8 (15a2-phase-3a-removals-part-2.md, lines 562-602) does plan this side-effect relocation into subscriptions.ts. However, Phase 3A is a separate plan that executes after Phase 2 — it has not yet landed and may not land before Phase 2C ships. The dependency is documented in prose only: no failing test, no CI gate, no merge-order enforcement between Phase 2C and Phase 3A.
affected_files: docs/composer/ux-redesign-2026-05/14c-phase-2c-frontend-integration.md (Task 8 binding constraint paragraph and Risks table: 'Removing handleValidate leaves injectSystemMessage/sendValidationFeedback untriggered')
blocking_issues.system_impact: If Phase 2C ships before Phase 3A Task 8 executes — which is the normal sequencing in a multi-phase rollout — validation-driven chat feedback (system message injection + sendValidationFeedback) silently stops firing. Validation events still occur (the audit-readiness panel fetches them), but the LLM no longer receives failure context in the chat channel. This breaks the LLM-assisted correction loop without any error signal.
blocking_issues.failure_mode: Silent capability removal. No error. Users lose LLM validation guidance with no indication of the loss.
blocking_issues.feedback_loop_risk: Balancing loop disrupted: the intended feedback is 'validation fails → LLM gets error context → LLM suggests fix → user fixes → validation passes'. Removing one link in that chain silently degrades the loop without the user or operator knowing.
recommended_fixes: Add a test in Phase 2C that imports subscriptions.ts and asserts that injectSystemMessage is called on validation failure. This test will FAIL if Phase 3A Task 8 has not landed, creating a mechanical gate.

Alternatively, Phase 2C should NOT delete the side effects from InspectorPanel.tsx at all; instead duplicate them in Phase 2C's panel-level handler and leave Phase 3A Task 8 to clean up the inspector copy. The duplication is safe (both subscriptions.ts and the inspector copy firing produces an extra message; that is observable but not harmful). Phase 3A then deletes the inspector copy as planned.

At minimum: add a TODO comment in InspectorPanel.tsx at the deletion site that causes a lint error (via a dedicated lint rule or an eslint-disable-next-line comment that is itself banned by the project eslint config) so the file cannot be committed without deliberate override.

warnings.title: EXTERNAL_CALL drift detection is unidirectional: plugins can make external calls without the marker
warnings.description: Phase 2A Task 2 includes a test (test_every_external_call_plugin_is_on_allowlist_or_explicitly_excepted) that catches the case where a Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL plugin is missing from the allowlist. The inverse is not tested: a plugin that makes external calls without the Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL marker will not be caught by this check. The closed-list approach trusts the marker entirely. A plugin author who forgets to set determinism=Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL on a new plugin that calls an external API will silently produce an incorrect audit-readiness panel (showing 'All plugins operate on pipeline data' for a plugin that crosses a Tier-3 boundary).
warnings.system_impact: The panel would present an incorrect audit claim. The impact is bounded to the panel's diagnostic accuracy — the actual audit trail is unaffected (external calls are still recorded). But the panel's purpose is to surface boundary crossings, and a missed one defeats that purpose.
warnings.recommendation: Document the unidirectionality explicitly in trust.py. For Phase 7 when data_trust_tier ClassVar lands, replace the marker-based check with a structural one that does not require developers to remember to set a flag.

warnings.title: No AbortController on fetch: stale snapshot can write after session navigation
warnings.description: Neither fetchAuditReadiness nor the store's loadSnapshot uses an AbortController. If a user navigates away from a session while a fetch is in flight, the fetch resolves and writes to the cache for the old sessionId. On return to that session, the panel may briefly render a stale snapshot before the version check triggers a refetch. The effect is cosmetic (a brief flash) but could be confusing during demo.
warnings.system_impact: Minor correctness exposure. Bounded by version-keyed eviction on the next composition change. Demo risk: if the session transition happens during a live demo, the audience may briefly see the wrong panel state.
warnings.recommendation: Pass an AbortController signal through fetchAuditReadiness. Store a per-session AbortController in the Zustand store and abort it in clearSession. This also mitigates the B1 race.

warnings.title: Validate button removal is a one-way UX change during demo-prep window
warnings.description: The standalone Validate button is removed in Phase 2C Task 8 and replaced by auto-validate on composition-version change. This is a one-way change. Memory project_rc5ux_demo_prep_scope states the active program is 'maximize success probability for upcoming tech demo'. If a demo audience member or evaluator expects to see a Validate button (because it was present in a prior demo or documentation), the absence is unexplained. The 'Audit ready' collapsed state on an all-green all-not_applicable composition (e.g., retention=not_applicable, llm_interpretations=not_applicable) could be misread as 'nothing to worry about' by an audience that does not know those rows exist.
affected_files: docs/composer/ux-redesign-2026-05/14-phase-2-audit-readiness-panel.md (Risks table: 'Removing the Validate button strands users mid-keystroke')
warnings.system_impact: Demo risk. Not a correctness issue. The panel design is sound for production; the question is whether landing it during demo-prep is strategically wise.
warnings.recommendation: Consider whether Phase 2C (button removal) should be deferred until after the demo, landing only Phase 2A and 2B (which add the panel without removing the button). The demo then shows two paths to validation — the panel and the button — with the button scheduled for removal post-demo. This preserves demo familiarity while demonstrating the new panel.

warnings.title: Three API modules duplicating getToken/authHeaders/parseResponse; Phase 8 deferral may not land
warnings.description: Phase 2B's auditReadiness.ts explicitly acknowledges duplicating getToken, authHeaders, and parseResponse from api/preferences.ts (14b §Task 2 Step 4 JSDoc comment: 'Technical debt: getToken/authHeaders/parseResponse are duplicated... Phase 8 cleanup task'). The pattern will be duplicated again by any future API module added before Phase 8. If Phase 8 slips or is descoped, the project retains four or more API modules with independently-evolving auth helpers.
warnings.system_impact: A change to the auth token mechanism (e.g., moving from localStorage to httpOnly cookies) requires N surgical edits across N modules instead of one. Risk increases with each phase that adds an API module.
warnings.recommendation: The Phase 8 deferral is documented and acknowledged. Acceptable if Phase 8 is treated as load-bearing, not optional. Flag this if Phase 8 shows signs of slipping during planning.

warnings.title: Phase 3B panel relocation dependency: prose-only constraint with no CI gate
warnings.description: 14c §Sequencing explicitly documents that Phase 3B Task 9 (15b2) must relocate AuditReadinessPanel from InspectorPanel.tsx to the SideRail's auditReadinessSlot BEFORE deleting InspectorPanel. 15b2 Task 9 Step 4a confirms this is planned (grep hit at 15b2 line 696). However the constraint is prose-only. If Phase 3B Task 9 runs without Step 4a (e.g., a sub-agent executes the git rm step before the relocation), the panel disappears from the UI silently.
warnings.system_impact: Panel disappears with no error. App launches normally. Demo would show no audit-readiness panel.
warnings.recommendation: 15b2 Task 9 Step 4a already includes a failing test that asserts AuditReadinessPanel renders inside the side rail post-task. Confirm that test is gated BEFORE the git rm step and that the vitest run is required to pass before the deletion commit. The plan notes this as CRITICAL; the sequencing within the task must enforce it, not just document it.

recommendations.title: Fix the loadSnapshot race before Phase 2B lands
recommendations.detail: Add an in-flight version tracker to auditReadinessStore: record the highest `compositionVersion` requested for each `sessionId`. In the resolution arm of loadSnapshot, before calling set(), check whether the snapshot's composition_version equals the tracked requested version. If it is lower, discard the result. This is a 5-line change to auditReadinessStore.ts and removes the false-positive 'Audit ready' scenario.

recommendations.title: Add a mechanical gate for Phase 2C Task 8's Phase 3A dependency
recommendations.detail: Phase 2C Task 8 should include a test that imports subscriptions.ts directly and asserts that it fires injectSystemMessage on a validation failure event. This test will fail if Phase 3A Task 8 has not landed (subscriptions.ts will not have the side-effect code). Without this gate, the prose-only constraint will be missed by an executor who follows the task list without reading the sequencing notes.

recommendations.title: Consider landing Phase 2A+2B for the demo; defer 2C (button removal) until post-demo
recommendations.detail: The panel adds value to the demo (shows audit thinking, introduces Linda's vocabulary). The button removal removes a familiar affordance under time pressure. A partial ship of 2A+2B gives 95% of the demo value with less risk. Phase 2C can land immediately after the demo as a cleanup commit.

recommendations.title: Add AbortController to both fetch functions
recommendations.detail: Wrap fetchAuditReadiness and fetchAuditReadinessExplain with a signal parameter. Store a per-session AbortController reference in auditReadinessStore and call abort() in clearSession(). This partially mitigates B1 (in-flight cancellation on session switch) and W2.

recommendations.title: Document EXTERNAL_CALL marker requirement in plugin authoring guide
recommendations.detail: Add a note to CLAUDE.md or a plugin authoring doc stating that any plugin making external HTTP/LLM/storage calls must set determinism=Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL. Without this, the trust.py test catches the inverse (allowlist drift) but not the forward case (missing marker).

cross_phase_coupling.coupling: Phase 2C Task 8 → Phase 3A Task 8 (subscriptions.ts side-effect relocation)
cross_phase_coupling.status: REAL but unguarded — 15a2 Task 8 plans the relocation in detail; the gap is the absence of a mechanical gate in Phase 2C

cross_phase_coupling.coupling: Phase 2C → Phase 3B Task 9 (panel relocation before InspectorPanel deletion)
cross_phase_coupling.status: REAL and gated by a failing test in 15b2 Task 9 Step 4a — dependency is mechanically enforced IF the test precedes the git rm step
cross_phase_coupling.risk: WARNING (see W5) — sequencing within 15b2 Task 9 must be verified

cross_phase_coupling.status: Correctly sequenced: 2A merges first, 2C smoke runs against deployed 2A routes
cross_phase_coupling.risk: LOW — if 2A fails staging, 2C smoke will surface it

cross_phase_coupling.status: One-line flip in _build_llm_interpretations_row (14a service.py); the Phase 5b deferral marker is explicit in the function body and the row builder is named distinctively

cross_phase_coupling.coupling: Phase 2 → Phase 7 (data_trust_tier ClassVar replacing closed allowlist)
cross_phase_coupling.status: Correctly decoupled — trust.py classifier is replaceable without wire-shape change

loops.description: Rapid composition edits → version bumps → concurrent validation fetches → race resolves with stale snapshot → panel shows false 'Audit ready' → user proceeds on incorrect audit state
Validation fails → LLM receives no error context (Phase 2C without Phase 3A) → LLM cannot suggest fix → validation stays failed → user manually investigates
compute_snapshot calls validate_pipeline on every composition version change; if validation is slow, user edits pile up behind in-flight fetches
loops.risk: LOW — plans correctly defer profiling; the version-keyed cache prevents redundant calls once a fetch completes

silent_failure_risks: Stale snapshot written to cache (B1) — panel shows wrong status with no indicator
handleValidate side effects lost if Phase 3A does not land before Phase 2C (B2)
Plugin making external calls without Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL marker silently mis-classified (W1)
loud_failure_risks: Subset-of-catalog test fails CI when allowlisted plugin name is renamed
EXTERNAL_CALL completeness test fails CI when Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL plugin is off allowlist
failure_mode_summary.idempotency_assessment: compute_snapshot is a read — naturally idempotent. loadSnapshot fire-and-forget pattern is NOT idempotent under concurrent calls with the same sessionId (multiple in-flight fetches fire before cache writes; see B1).

timing_assumption_analysis.assumption: Phase 3A Task 8 has landed before Phase 2C ships
timing_assumption_analysis.what_breaks: injectSystemMessage and sendValidationFeedback stop firing on validation events

timing_assumption_analysis.assumption: cached.composition_version === compositionVersion guard prevents concurrent fetches
timing_assumption_analysis.what_breaks: Two concurrent calls at the same session both pass the guard (cache is empty), both fire; stale resolver overwrites fresh cache

timing_assumption_analysis.assumption: Phase 2A is deployed before Phase 2C staging smoke
timing_assumption_analysis.what_breaks: Smoke fails with 404 on audit-readiness routes
timing_assumption_analysis.severity: LOW — correctly documented as a prerequisite

timing_assumption_analysis.assumption: Phase 3B Task 9 Step 4a (panel relocation) precedes the git rm InspectorPanel step
timing_assumption_analysis.severity: WARNING — see W5; gated by a test in 15b2 but sequencing must be verified

value_items: Panel surfaces audit thinking in real time — directly demonstrates ELSPETH's core value proposition
Collapsed 'Audit ready' with one-click expand is a clean, tidy demo moment
Explain dialog provides a printable narrative — suitable for showing to a non-technical audience
risk_items: Validate button removal removes a familiar affordance under demo time pressure (W3)
All-green / all-not_applicable collapse may read as 'nothing to worry about' to an audience unfamiliar with the row model
B1 race can fire visibly during rapid live editing in front of an audience
demo_readiness_assessment.recommendation: Land Phase 2A + Phase 2B for the demo. Defer Phase 2C (button removal, panel mount in InspectorPanel, full sub-components) until post-demo. This delivers ~80% of the demo value at lower risk.
